key_insight: A two-stage parametric FEM for constrained Helfrich flow decouples energy-stable normal-velocity computation from geometry-correcting mesh motion, preserving both dissipation and exact constraints.
engineering_relevance: This gives computational mechanics practitioners a robust way to simulate evolving curves/surfaces with guaranteed energy decay, exact volume/area conservation, and good mesh quality.
method: Stage 1: solve a linear parametric FEM for normal velocity from a curvature evolution equation with soft volume/area constraints.
Stage 2: update the mesh using the computed normal velocity plus a BGN-type tangential velocity.
Apply a time-weighted interface normal and area-correction multiplier; solve the resulting nonlinear correction with Newton iteration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on 2D curves and 3D surfaces, including nonsmooth initial data and nonzero spontaneous curvature, to verify accuracy and structure-preserving behavior.
watch_item: The constraint-enforcing correction step is nonlinear and Newton-based, so robustness and cost may depend on initialization, timestep size, and mesh quality.
paper_title: Geometric structure-preserving parametric finite element approximations for the constrained Helfrich flow
